document conventions include using stand: Document conventions include using standard naming schemes (e.g., snake_case), consistent formatting across sections with clear headings/subheadings in bold font type; tables should be presented as ASCII art or plain text, and figures must have captions that describe their content accurately without relying on color coding for differentiation purposes.
user classes are divided into three main: User classes are divided into three main categories: Administrators (with full system access), Librarians/Catalogers (limited to resource management tasks such as adding new items or updating existing ones) and Patrons (restricted from any administrative functions). Each class has specific roles, responsibilities, privileges granted based on their job function within the library environment.
the system should be compatible with win: The system should be compatible with Windows Server 2008 R2/Windows Vista or higher versions of operating systems due to its reliance on .NET Framework for development purposes; it must also support SQL Server Express Edition as a database management solution and have the capability to interface seamlessly via SOAP web services.
design constraints include ensuring that: Design constraints include ensuring that all administrative tasks can be performed remotely through secure connections (HTTPS), maintaining data integrity during backup/restore operations, providing multi-language support for international libraries with diverse user bases; the system must also adhere to accessibility standards such as WCAG 2.0 Level AA guidelines and comply with relevant legal regulations like GDPR or FERPA depending on geographical location of deployment.
implementation constraints involve using: Implementation constraints involve using C# for development language due its robustness in handling complex data structures required by library systems; the system should be designed to accommodate future expansion such as adding new modules/features without significant rework, ensuring scalability and maintaining performance under heavy loads during peak usage times.
the system administration module will re: The System Administration Module will replace existing commercial ILSes, enhance their capabilities, and add new functionality within a full-featured ILS framework.
to manage all aspects of an integrated l: To manage all aspects of an integrated library system (ILS).
this srs is for king county library's il: This SRS is for King County Library's ILS and can be adapted to other similar systems.
the system administration module must su: The System Administration Module must support configuring the ILS to enable features required for management of Library branches.
the module should allow configuration se: The module should allow configuration settings related to patrons' data handling processes.
it needs capabilities for managing colle: It needs capabilities for managing collection and circulation transaction records within the system.
provisioning services, ports, and applic: Provisioning services, ports, and application programming interfaces must be monitorable by System Administration Module users.
the ability to manage server software in: The ability to manage server software installation, upgrades, and updates is required for the system administration tasks.
database backup functionalities must inc: Database backup functionalities must include support for backing up databases, configuration files, log files etc., as part of routine maintenance activities.
the software should be understandable by: The software should be understandable by non-technical users.
software development will occur within a: Software development will occur within a collaborative and iterative environment with end-user involvement.
patron a can access library resources re: Patron A can access library resources regardless of their location or card status.
staff members must be able to design and: Staff members must be able to design and provision services for patrons.
the system administration module relies: The System Administration Module relies on data structures and functionality from an enterprise-level Library Automation System, including Acquisitions and Cataloging modules.
the system administration module interfa: The System Administration Module interface with a variety of vendor websites via published APIs and/or automated transfer of standard-format data files (e.g., USMARC21, EDIFACT).
the srs document includes process flowch: The SRS document includes process flowcharts for contextual information, not as prescriptive guides.
patron a is a customer who may possess o: Patron A is a customer who may possess or lack a library card, be on-site or off-site, using print materials, media materials, or electronic resources.
staff include managers, librarians, tech: Staff include managers, librarians, technicians, assistants, and pages involved in service design and provisioning.
library-pecific software documentation f: Library-pecific software documentation following standard technical formats is web-based and indexed by function with easy searchability reflecting the specific version of the system in use at library (related processes not specified).
system upgrades and updates include writ: System upgrades and updates include written guidelines for updating servers and clients. Includes list of new, changed, and removed features.
System provides access to root shell.
system provides full access to all log f: System provides full access to all log files. Logs can be enabled, disabled, and set to a specific retention threshold without stopping the system.
system supports monitoring of performanc: System supports monitoring of performance using SNMP
system allows creation of custom dashboa: System allows creation of custom dashboards that display current and historical data about system performance, record creation and modification, circulation transactions, etc.
ability to set thresholds on the length: Ability to set thresholds on the length of time records are locked and provide a list of all record types in sustained use/locked condition. Ability from the same console to unlock one or more records.
system provides dashboard for performanc: System provides dashboard for performance monitoring with identification of processes by process ID, owner username, IP address (if applicable), CPU utilization, memory usage and run time. Identification of runaway processes is included along with system status represented visually via green/red lights.
server management console provided which: Server management console provided which includes software shutdown utility, startup utility, server shutdown utility, restart utility among others.
circulation dashboard showing key perfor: Circulation dashboard showing key performance indicators such as check-outs per hour, check-ins per hour, holds placed per hour, and holds paged per day. Indicators can be limited to a single branch or set systemwide.
email configuration (quick menu for comm: Email configuration (quick menu for common settings)
business rules restrictions on record de: Business rules restrictions on record deletion/modification based on status or holidays
system allows creation and modification: System allows creation and modification of requesting rules that determine whether a patron can place a hold on an item based on various criteria

System allows creation and modification of loan rules to allow or disallow check-out, calculate loan periods, and set renewal limits with evaluations such as patron type, number of items checked out, account balance, etc.
ability to specify default value, data v: Ability to specify default value, data validation, automatic formatting, and required status for any field
system logs data changes (such as record: System logs data changes (such as record deletions) and provides "undo" functionality, with ideal revision control capabilities.
the product scope includes features such: The product scope includes features such as account creation/modification
resource management (books, journals) sh: Resource management (books, journals) should be possible
backup & restore functions should be ava: Backup & restore functions should be available
integration with external systems like c: Integration with external systems like cataloging databases or interlibrary loan services should be possible
the system should provide a comprehensiv: The system should provide a comprehensive set of tools for managing user accounts, permissions, system settings, and library resources
only authorized personnel (librarians, i: Only authorized personnel (librarians, IT administrators) should have administrative access to the System Administration module
operating environment: the system should: Operating Environment: The system should be compatible with Windows Server 2008 R2/Windows Vista or higher versions of operating systems due to its reliance on .NET Framework for development purposes; it must also support SQL Server Express Edition as a database management solution and have the capability to interface seamlessly via SOAP web services.
the system administration module must pr: The System Administration module must provide a user-friendly interface with intuitive navigation for non-technical staff members while offering advanced functionality through customizable views or reports tailored to specific administrative tasks; it should also include comprehensive error handling mechanisms that log detailed information about any issues encountered during operation along with automated alerts/notifications sent via email upon critical failures.
the system must support multi-user acces: The system must support multi-user access concurrently without data corruption, ensuring transactional integrity through ACID (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) properties; it should also provide robust security measures such as role-based access control mechanisms and encryption for sensitive information stored within the database.
the system must be able to handle large: The system must be able to handle large volumes of data efficiently with minimal latency during search/query operations using indexing techniques like B+ trees or hash indexes depending on query patterns observed; it should also support batch processing capabilities allowing administrators perform bulk updates, deletions etc., without impacting overall performance significantly.
the system must provide comprehensive au: The system must provide comprehensive audit logging functionality that records all administrative actions performed by users along with timestamps and user identities for future reference or troubleshooting purposes; it should also support export/import of logs in various formats such as CSV, XML etc., allowing administrators to analyze trends over time.
the system must include backup & restore: The system must include backup & restore functionality that allows administrators perform regular backups at scheduled intervals with options like full, incremental or differential based on user preferences; it should also provide a secure method for storing these backups offsite/cloud storage solutions along with verification mechanisms ensuring data integrity post- restoration.
user account management features includi: User account management features including creation, modification, deletion of user accounts should be supported in this module.
the system administration module relies: The System Administration Module relies on the data structures and functionality of an enterprise-level Library Automation System, including Acquisitions and Cataloging modules.
library-pecific software documentation f: Library-pecific software documentation following standard technical formats is web-based and indexed by function with easy searchability reflecting the specific version of the system in use at library (related processes not specified)
system supports monitoring of performanc: System supports monitoring of performance using SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol)
system allows creation of custom dashboa: System allows creation of custom dashboards that display current and historical data about system performance, record creation and modification, circulation transactions, etc. Administrators can create these dashboards and grant access to selected users or groups.
system allows creation and modification: System allows creation and modification of requesting rules that determine whether a patron can place a hold on an item based on various criteria.
system provides capability to perform li: System provides capability to perform live incremental and full backups of data and transaction logs.
user account privileges must allow syste: User account privileges must allow system administrative staff full visibility and control over user privileges.
patron data security requires that patro: Patron data security requires that patron data is secure in all transfers to and from the system, with related requirements including MARC import/export (Req ID: 1716) for bibliographic records management.

The chargeback request results in a printed chargeback notification that is sent to merchant indicating that their account will be charged back if no proof is provided that the disputed transaction is valid.

REQ_3: The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of a chargeback request. The confirmation must contain the following information; the dispute case number, the type of chargeback requested (pending or immediate), and the date that the merchant response is due.

REQ_4: The Disputes System shall prevent the creation of duplicate dispute requests 100% of the time.

REQ_5: The Disputes System shall accurately provide a list of possible reason codes for a given transaction. The list of possible reason codes must be derived based on the type of transaction and the industry that the merchant belongs to according to the business rules defined for the Disputes System.

REQ_6: The Disputes System must provide the ability to view existing dispute cases.

REQ_7: The Disputes System must provide Search functionality. The search method must include the ability to search by (1) the dispute case number, (2) the merchant account number, (3) the cardmember account number, and (4) the issuer number. In addition to the above criteria, the search functionality must further allow the user to limit the results of the search by a date range, the type of dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification), the case status (open, closed, or all), and the dispute reason code.

REQ_8: A list of all disputes cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color-coding the line item in the list. For example, all open cases will be yellow and all closed cases will be gray.

REQ_9: The Disputes System shall provide the users the ability to view the history of all activities that have occurred on the dispute case.

REQ_10: The Dispute System shall provide the users with the ability to follow up on a dispute case via follow-up actions. The list of available follow-up actions for a given dispute case must be filtered by the status of the case and the access level of the user.

Here are the software requirements extracted from the text:

ID|Requirement
1|The system shall always echo with special characters representing typed characters.
2|The customer's web browser shall never display a customer's credit card number after retrieving from the database. It shall always be shown with just the last 4 digits of the credit card number.
3|The system's back-end servers shall never display a customer's password. The customer's password may be reset but never shown.
4|The system's back-end databases shall be encrypted.
5|The system shall allow the user to register for an account from any page in the system.
6|The system will use a single password per user to login to the E-Store System.
7|The system shall use secure socket layer (SSL) technology to ensure all payments made online and user logins are secure.
8|Any page of the application should not take more than 6 seconds to load on a DSL broadband connection.
9|The system may be throttled or slowed down on heavy loads to ensure service for everybody. By throttling is meant that certain functionality may be unavailable during heavy server load.
10|The application should be able to support 100 concurrent users without any performance degradation.
11|The system should be able to scale up to 500 concurrent users (if there is a need in the future) by installing additional hardware components.
12|The MTBF (if any) should not be less than 2 months.
13|A secure server will be required to ensure confidentiality of customer's credit card and other details.
14|The user interface of the system should be very user friendly.
15|It should not take more than 120 seconds for a new user to register for an account.
16|It should not take more than 90 seconds for a registered user to place an order.
17|The system should be easy enough to maintain that someone else could do it with a manual and a few hours training.
18|The system should be portable to various operating environments.
19|Registered User must be able to maintain his/her session information for at least 60 minutes of inactive session before the system prompts him to log out of the system. The registered user must be provided with all the options of the E-store regardless of the time when he/she logs in.
20|The entire website must be user-friendly and easily navigable. The website must be provided with a site map for quick access to a particular link according to the requirement specification. The user must be able to find what he/she wants from the site without any difficulty. The website must adhere to branding schemes and the layout of the web pages must be uniform throughout.
21|The system shall support up to 10000 simultaneous users against the central database at any given time and up to 5000 simultaneous users against the local servers at any one time. The performance of the website must be optimal in case of huge loads and hence appropriate load balancing must be done to achieve this.
